description: This slow cooker beef stew transforms simple ingredients into a hearty, soul-warming meal that’s perfect for busy weeknights or weekend family gatherings. Packed with tender beef, nutritious vegetables, and rich flavors, this recipe delivers restaurant-quality results with minimal effort.
ingredients: [{"name": "beef chuck roast", "unit": "pounds", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "medium carrots", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "Yukon Gold potatoes", "unit": "medium", "amount": "3"}, {"name": "yellow onions", "unit": "large", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "fresh garlic", "unit": "cloves", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "low-sodium beef broth", "unit": "cups", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "fresh thyme", "unit": "teaspoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "bay leaves", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "olive oil", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "2"}]
instructions: Cut beef chuck roast into uniform 1-inch cubes. Dice onions, chop carrots, cube potatoes, and mince garlic. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown beef cubes on all sides, working in batches to avoid overcrowding. This takes about 6-8 minutes total. Transfer browned beef to slow cooker. Add diced onions, minced garlic, chopped carrots, and cubed potatoes. Stir gently to distribute evenly. Pour beef broth until ingredients are just covered. Add fresh thyme leaves, bay leaves, salt, and pepper. Stir once more to combine all elements. Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4 hours. The beef should be fork-tender and vegetables should be easily pierced. Remove bay leaves before serving. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper as needed. Serve immediately while hot.
notes: Pat beef cubes completely dry before searing to achieve better browning and prevent oil splatter. Don’t skip the searing step – it adds incredible depth of flavor that can’t be replicated any other way. Cut vegetables slightly larger than you think necessary as they’ll shrink during the long cooking process. For thicker stew, mix 2 tablespoons cornstarch with cold water and stir in during the last 30 minutes.
tags: slow cooker beef stew, beef stew recipe, slow cooker recipes, comfort food, easy dinner
